digests.narrative:
Photonics is his deepest-tracked, highest-conviction theme — he's actively repositioning into it. The organizing idea is an ~8-month pricing lead: next-gen 1.6T pluggables (LITE, COHR, Innolight) are inflecting now, with AAOI and JBL's 1.6T LRO as H1-2027 "later bloomers," CPO scale-out in late H2 2026 (POET, Shunsin) and CPO scale-up out in H2 2027.

He calls AAOI around $150 "super compelling" and JBL around $370 "very compelling," while leaning toward COHR as the core holding and debating adding JBL. He prefers names that span many architectures — SIVEF (pluggable + NPO + CPO) is a favorite. After a sharp run (AAOI $40->$150) he expects range-bound chop until next earnings, and is cautious on POET over US quantum-dot dilution.
keyPoints: 8-month pricing rule: 1.6T pluggables (LITE/COHR) now; AAOI/JBL 1.6T LRO H1 2027; CPO scale-up H2 2027.
AAOI ~$150 "super compelling" and JBL ~$370 "very compelling"; COHR is the core, weighing adding JBL.
Favors architecture-spanning names — SIVEF covers pluggable, NPO and CPO.
Expects range-bound chop until next earnings after the big run-up (AAOI $40->$150).
Cautious on POET due to US quantum-dot R&D dilution risk.

digests.narrative:
He frames memory as a "massive bottleneck" and one of his core repositioning themes. HBM3e is shipping now (Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix), HBM4 is entering mass production, and HBF is a 2027 story — all driven by the same oligopoly (MU, SK Hynix, Samsung, Kioxia) that he expects to "keep compounding" while the market re-rates them mid-bottleneck.

He's holding his Korea/memory exposure (EWY LEAPs up triple digits) rather than adding, and hunts the upstream chokepoints around it: AXTI (InP substrates), the tungsten/WF6 supply scare into DRAM/NAND, and indium/MLCC controls. The small-cap angle he's watching is Auros, an SK Hynix/Samsung HBM4 hybrid-bonding supplier qualifying in H2 for a 2027 ramp.
keyPoints: Memory is a "massive bottleneck": HBM3e shipping, HBM4 ramping, HBF ~2027.
Same oligopoly (MU, SK Hynix/000660.KS, Samsung, Kioxia) re-rating mid-bottleneck -> keeps compounding.
Holding Korea/EWY memory exposure (triple-digit gains) but not adding more.
Upstream chokepoints: AXTI InP substrates, tungsten/WF6 and indium export controls.
Watching tiny HBM4 hybrid-bonding supplier Auros (H2 qualification -> 2027 ramp).

digests.narrative:
Fabs are the backbone of nearly every note — more derisked compounders than explosive bets. TSM is the centerpiece: he sees it building a silicon-photonics empire (COUPE / CoPoS via subsidiaries Xintec and VisEra), with 2-3 year volumes implying 2028 and pilot lines already running, plus a "sleeper" margin story at ~$1.9T from roughly 2% uplift on price hikes.

INTC remains a "great hold" on the made-in-America foundry thesis and EMIB advanced packaging, and TSEM has already re-rated hundreds of percent with more room — he'd "pyramid" winners rather than take short-term gains. INTC even makes his "3 stocks to forget for 10 years" list (with RKLB and SIVE).
keyPoints: TSM building a silicon-photonics empire (COUPE/CoPoS via Xintec/VisEra); pilot lines now, volumes imply 2028.
TSM a "sleeper" at ~$1.9T on ~2% margin uplift from price hikes — derisked.
INTC a "great hold": made-in-America foundry + EMIB advanced packaging.
TSEM already re-rated hundreds of %; prefers to "pyramid" winners over taking short-term gains.
Backbone holds — more priced-in than photonics/memory, not the highest-upside bucket.

digests.narrative:
Neoclouds is one of his four repositioning themes, but a timing-sensitive one. NBIS is the lead: he expects its $7-9B ARR projection to show up at Q4 2026 earnings — "the big showtime" — which is when neocloud leaders typically inflect.

He admits being early (focused too hard in H4 2025); by his 8-month rule the buy window was ~March 2026, and the market may not price the move until a Feb 2027 window. Still a buildout phase, so he treats it as accumulate-into-inflection rather than a chase.
keyPoints: One of four repositioning themes (Memory, Neoclouds, Photonics, Robotics).
NBIS the lead: $7-9B ARR should surface at Q4 2026 earnings ("the big showtime").
Admits being early (H4 2025); 8-month rule pointed to ~March 2026, market may price Feb 2027.
Still buildout phase — accumulate into the Q4 2026 inflection rather than chase.

digests.narrative:
His materials/packaging angle runs on one framework: equipment and machine suppliers hit revenue before the volume ramp does, because fabs need the machines first. That's why he took LPK positions. AEHR is the watch item — more priced-in after a 100% rally, but he expects volume orders soon to drive another ~30% leg.

Around that he tracks the mini-shifts: advanced-packaging and hybrid-bonding materials for memory, MLCC and other upstream bottlenecks, and substrate dynamics (Soitec / SOI.PA) framed through monopoly and licensing lenses.
keyPoints: Framework: equipment/machine suppliers (LPK.DE) book revenue before the volume ramp — he's long LPK.
AEHR more priced-in after a 100% rally; expects volume orders soon -> another ~30% leg.
Plays mini-shifts: advanced-packaging / hybrid-bonding materials and MLCC chokepoints.
Substrate angle via Soitec (SOI.PA), framed through monopoly/licensing dynamics.

digests.narrative:
"Robotics is next." He points to deal count and investment at all-time highs (a16z / PitchBook) and plays it early, mostly through proxies and cross-exposure rather than pure-plays — TSLA (Optimus) and large programs inside names like AMZN, since most pure humanoid names are still pre-IPO.

His angle: AI-datacenter exposure often carries humanoid cross-exposure (memory for inference, DFB lasers for FMCW LiDAR vision). Like Space, he expects the market to price it in early, with a global humanoid IPO wave H2 into 2027.
keyPoints: "Robotics is next" — deal count and investment at all-time highs (a16z/PitchBook).
Plays it early via proxies/cross-exposure (TSLA Optimus, AMZN programs); pure-plays still pre-IPO.
AI-DC names often carry humanoid cross-exposure (memory for inference, DFB lasers for LiDAR).
Like Space, expects early pricing-in; global humanoid IPO wave H2 -> 2027.

digests.narrative:
He treats NVDA (and AVGO) less as the trade and more as a map to under-covered ecosystem suppliers. The method: read Goldman-style ecosystem notes and hunt the names NOT listed — the "hidden gems" where the highest returns hide — like MSSCorp (a near-monopoly in CPO inspection at ~$1.2B) and Nextronics (NVDA CPO connectors and cage thermal, still ~$200m).

He's rotating into purer-play ecosystem names ahead of the H2 volume ramp, and passes on those likely to be designed out later (e.g., HIMX).
keyPoints: NVDA/AVGO used as a map to under-covered CPO ecosystem suppliers, not just the trade.
Method: scan Goldman ecosystem notes for names NOT listed — the "hidden gems."
Examples: MSSCorp (~monopoly CPO inspection), Nextronics (NVDA CPO connectors/thermal).
Rotating into purer-play names ahead of H2 volume ramp; passes on likely design-outs (HIMX).
